title: Space-Time Tradeoffs Spectrum
description: A graph illustrating the inverse relationship between the space (memory) used by an algorithm and the time (computation) it takes to execute, with common algorithmic examples plotted along the spectrum.
relationships: As available memory (space) increases, execution time tends to decrease, and vice versa. Techniques like lookup tables and caching use more space to save time, while techniques like compression use more time to save space.
